english: My practice test scores aren't great.
alt: I'm not doing well on the mock exams.

alt: Practicing with previous test papers works best.

korean: 시험 보러 가는 길에 연필 깜빡했어

english: I scored higher than I expected and I'm thrilled.
alt: My score was better than I thought. I'm so happy.

alt: Apparently the class average was terrible.

alt: Reviewing your mistakes in a notebook is super useful.

alt: I couldn't put this book down. Read it cover to cover.

english: Writing things out by hand works best for me.
alt: I'm the type who memorizes better by handwriting.

english: I focus better when I study at a coffee shop.
alt: Studying at a cafe helps me concentrate.

english: Pull out the key points and make a summary note.
alt: Just condense the main ideas into a cheat sheet.

korean: 혼자 공부하는 것보다 같이 하면 더 잘 돼
english: Studying with others works better for me than studying alone.
alt: I'm more productive when I study with someone.

alt: Saying things out loud makes them easier to remember.
note: read out loud = 소리 내어 읽다 / stick = 기억에 남다

english: I was cramming so hard before the exam, I didn't even eat.

korean: 유튜브 공부법 영상 보다가 시간 다 갔어

alt: I wasted hours watching study method videos instead of studying.

alt: Using flashcards to learn words is really effective.

korean: 도서관에서 공부하면 분위기 때문에 더 집중 돼
english: The library atmosphere helps me focus better.
alt: I concentrate better at the library because of the environment.
note: atmosphere = environment = 분위기

korean: 밑줄만 치고 넘어가지 말고 이해하면서 읽어
english: Don't just underline and move on—actually read to understand.
alt: Don't passively highlight; actively comprehend the material.
note: actively comprehend = 능동적으로 이해하다

korean: 잠자기 전에 한 번 더 보면 장기 기억에 좋대
english: Reviewing before bed is supposed to help with long-term memory.
alt: They say going over material before sleep boosts retention.

english: It's better to go through one workbook multiple times.
alt: Repeating a single workbook beats doing several once.
note: workbook = 문제집 / beats = ~보다 낫다

korean: 시험 전에 친구랑 서로 문제 내면서 공부했어
english: We quizzed each other before the exam.
alt: My friend and I tested each other to study.
note: quiz each other = 서로 문제를 내다

korean: 공부할 때 핸드폰 멀리 두는 게 최고야
english: The best thing is to keep your phone far away while studying.
alt: Put your phone out of reach when you study.

english: Having a study plan makes me way more efficient.
alt: Planning my study sessions has boosted my productivity.
note: study plan = 공부 계획 / boost productivity = 생산성을 높이다

english: What's your major?
alt: What are you studying?
note: major = 전공 / What are you studying?도 전공을 물어볼 때 자주 씀

english: You need to take a lot of credits if you want to double major.
alt: Double majoring means a heavier course load.

english: I got a scholarship so I don't have to worry about tuition.
alt: My scholarship covers tuition so that's a relief.

english: Have you fulfilled all the graduation requirements?
alt: Did you meet all the requirements to graduate?

english: I'm torn between going to grad school and getting a job.
alt: I can't decide whether to pursue a master's or start working.
note: grad school = graduate school = 대학원 / torn = 갈등하는

korean: 논문 주제 정하는 게 제일 어려워
english: Choosing a thesis topic is the hardest part.
alt: The toughest thing about a thesis is picking the subject.

english: Tuition was so expensive I had to take out a student loan.
alt: I had to borrow money because the tuition was too high.

english: I need to ask my professor for a letter of recommendation.
alt: I have to request a reference letter from my professor.

english: I took a year off from school.
alt: I went on a leave of absence for a year.

english: Coming back to school after a break, it's hard to readjust.
alt: Returning after my leave of absence has been tough.
note: readjust = 재적응하다 / return = 복학하다

english: My thesis deadline is right around the corner.
alt: The due date for my dissertation is fast approaching.
note: right around the corner = 코앞인 / fast approaching = 빠르게 다가오는

korean: 전공 바꾸고 싶은데 너무 늦은 것 같아
english: I want to switch my major, but I think it's too late.

english: What's your minor?
alt: What are you minoring in?
note: minor = 부전공

english: Credits from exchange programs are recognized.
alt: Study abroad credits transfer over.

english: How many semesters do you have left until graduation?
alt: When do you graduate?
note: semester = 학기 / graduate = 졸업하다

english: Have you taken a campus tour?
alt: Did you get a chance to look around campus?
note: campus tour = 캠퍼스 투어

english: How's dorm life?
alt: What's living in the dorms like?
note: dorm = dormitory = 기숙사

korean: 대학원 입시 준비가 취업 준비만큼 힘들어
english: Preparing for grad school admissions is as hard as job hunting.
alt: Applying to grad school is just as tough as finding a job.
note: admissions = 입학 / job hunting = 취업 준비

korean: 석사 논문 쓰느라 잠을 못 잤어
english: I couldn't sleep because I was working on my master's thesis.
alt: I've been losing sleep over my master's thesis.
note: master's thesis = 석사 논문

english: When is the graduation ceremony?
alt: What's the date for commencement?

korean: 조교 하면 등록금 면제 받을 수 있어
english: If you work as a teaching assistant, you can get a tuition waiver.
alt: Being a TA comes with a tuition waiver.

english: Undergrad and grad school are two completely different worlds.
alt: Graduate school is a whole different ball game from undergrad.
note: undergrad = 학부 / a different ball game = 완전 다른 것

korean: 인턴십 하면서 수업도 들으려니 체력이 안 돼
english: Doing an internship and taking classes at the same time is physically exhausting.

english: I have no idea what to do after graduation.

note: career path = 진로 / clueless = 전혀 모르는

english: My advisor asked for more revisions on my thesis.

korean: 새 학기라 교재 사느라 돈이 많이 들었어
